question.text: The Know Your Customer (KYC) process for mutual fund investments needs to be completed:

answer.text: Only once, at the time of the first mutual fund or other capital market transaction
Separately for each mutual fund scheme purchased
Every year at the time of annual renewal
Each time the investor makes a new purchase transaction

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** KYC needs to be done only once at the time of the first mutual fund or other capital market transaction, and is valid across mutual funds and other capital market intermediaries. The KYC procedure involves collecting investor information and conducting In-Person Verification (IPV). SEBI has also allowed Aadhaar-based eKYC for MF investments.
**The Trap:** KYC is NOT scheme-specific or transaction-specific — once completed, it is valid across all mutual funds and capital market intermediaries. No annual renewal is required for KYC in mutual funds (though some updates may be needed for FATCA compliance).
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. KYC norms are also relevant in Series VIII (Equity Derivatives), Series XIII (Common Derivatives) and other capital market exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Simplify the KYC process for new investors by explaining it is a one-time requirement. Recommend Aadhaar-based eKYC which eliminates paperwork and IPV entirely, making the onboarding process quick and hassle-free.
